system_description.overview: Human Chemical is an AI-powered predictive toxicology platform for industrial chemicals, pioneering the use of QSAR/bioactivity models to predict chemical toxicity. The platform provides an agentic AI framework for regulatory summaries, citation management, and a copilot interface for chemical safety workflows.

products.Predictive Toxicology Models: QSAR and bioactivity-based AI models for industrial chemical toxicity prediction
products.Agentic AI Framework: Automated citations, regulatory summaries, and AI copilot for chemical safety analysis
products.Custom Modeling Support: Bespoke modeling services for customer-specific chemical safety requirements

infrastructure.cloud_provider: GCP (carve-out — GCP is a subservice organization)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with network ACLs; production and staging Kubernetes clusters fully separated
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls on application gateway and production network; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management; GCP security groups and network ACLs
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS/IPS via GCP (intrusion detection and prevention tools configured)
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet → GKE Load Balancer → Production Kubernetes Cluster (us-central-1, regional): prd frontend app → prd backend app → prd Postgres Cloud SQL + Google Key Manager. Staging Kubernetes Cluster (us-central-1, regional): stg frontend app → stg backend app → stg Postgres Cloud SQL + Google Key Manager. Management access via SSH bastion host.

compute: GKE (Google Kubernetes Engine) — production and staging clusters
application_architecture.environments: Production and staging GKE clusters fully separated; development/test environments separate from production

databases: Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL) — separate production and staging instances
data_storage.backup_frequency: Regular (formal backup policy; specifics not stated beyond 'suitable backups taken and maintained')

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: GCP (Active Directory, server and database accounts; GCP password policy enforced)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; SCM access requires MFA
authentication_access_control.password_policy: GCP password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; lockout after failed login attempts; biometrics also supported for desktop unlock

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Google Key Manager implied)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: SCM (name not disclosed; MFA enforced; source code changes logged, timestamped, attributed)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge to production requires additional approval; branch protection rules configured; developers cannot push directly to production
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as code; configuration management tool for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management; CTO performs monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (name not disclosed) with predefined threshold alerts; continuous infrastructure performance monitoring
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level; log access restricted; logs retained per policy
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; alerts tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure — compute (GKE), database (Cloud SQL), key management (Google Key Manager), networking, security (IDS/IPS)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; weekly AV definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning; self-assessment scan prior to every production release; annual scan

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — physical access delegated to GCP (subservice); GCP responsible for data center security
A1.2 — environmental protections delegated to GCP and building management
Processing Integrity criteria — not relevant to system
Privacy criteria — not relevant to system
notable_controls: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) role formally assigned for incident response oversight
Vendor management includes quarterly risk assessment for non-SOC vendors
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Multi-AZ DR by spinning up servers across all GCP availability zones in us-central-1
Formal Change Management policy with emergency change process
Monthly patch management reviews by CTO

infrastructure.rationale: GCP regional Kubernetes cluster (GKE) in us-central-1, multi-AZ, separate production and staging clusters, IaC baseline configs, Google Key Manager. Single region only, no CDN, no WAF — limits score.

application_architecture.rationale: GKE microservices with separate production and staging clusters, branch protection with required approvals, per-tenant segregation. No named languages/frameworks, no named CI/CD tool.

data_layer.rationale: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with separate production and staging instances, Google Key Manager encryption, multi-AZ. No caching layer, no file storage, no data warehouse. Backup policy exists but frequency not quantified.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS via GCP, monthly vuln scans + pre-release self-assessment + annual scan, annual external pen test, antivirus (weekly definitions), MFA for admin access, content filtering, CISO role defined, firewall rules reviewed annually. No WAF named, no SIEM, no cybersecurity insurance disclosed.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with rollback capability, GKE orchestration, separate staging/production clusters, branch protection, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews, daily patch compliance scans. CI/CD tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy, annual review and testing across multiple loss scenarios confirmed, multi-AZ failover by spinning up GCP servers across all AZs. No stated RTO/RPO, no multi-region DR, backup frequency not quantified beyond 'suitable backups'.

vendor_diversity.rationale: GCP is the sole critical infrastructure provider — compute, database, networking, key management, and IDS/IPS all delegated to GCP. No secondary cloud provider or significant third-party services named beyond GCP.

overall.rationale: Clean SOC 2 Type 1 with zero exceptions and solid GCP-native architecture. GKE microservices with production/staging separation, CISO role, quarterly access reviews, annual pen testing. Key gaps: no WAF, single cloud provider (GCP) and single region, no RTO/RPO, very small team (6 people), no cybersecurity insurance disclosed, backup frequency unquantified.

red_flags: No WAF disclosed — application layer attack protection gap
No RTO/RPO stated — availability commitments unquantified
Heavy GCP concentration — compute, database, key management, IDS/IPS all dependent on single provider
Very small team (CEO, COO, CTO, CoS, 2 Senior Engineers + Chairman) — key person risk for security operations
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool and monitoring platform not named
No CDN mentioned — potential latency for distributed customers
No multi-region failover — DR limited to multi-AZ within us-central-1
Security criteria only — Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, Processing Integrity not audited
Backup frequency not explicitly quantified ('suitable backups' only)
No named file storage or caching layer — data architecture opacity
green_flags: SOC 2 Type 1 with zero exceptions across 29 controls
GKE microservices with fully separate production and staging Kubernetes clusters
Google Key Manager (KMS) for encryption at rest
CISO role formally assigned for incident response oversight
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning + pre-release self-assessment + annual scan (triple-layer)
Quarterly access reviews with 1-business-day revocation SLA
MFA enforced for all admin/production access and SCM
IaC with rollback capability via configuration management tool
BCDR tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Multi-AZ failover across all GCP availability zones in us-central-1
Vendor management with quarterly risk assessment for non-SOC vendors
key_observations: Human Chemical operates in predictive toxicology for industrial chemicals — regulatory data (REACH, EPA Toxic Substances Control Act) likely handled, making data integrity and access controls high-stakes
Extremely small team of 6 people (CEO, COO, CTO, CoS, 2 Senior Engineers) — key person concentration risk; CTO personally owns patch management and security reviews
GCP regional Kubernetes cluster (not GKE Autopilot) shows direct infrastructure engineering capability
Triple-layer vulnerability scanning (monthly automated + pre-release self-assessment + annual) is stronger than typical for a company this size
CISO role formally documented (referenced in CC7.4/CC2.1.4) despite tiny team — suggests compliance-aware culture
No AI model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, GCP Vertex) named — QSAR/bioactivity models appear to be proprietary rather than LLM-based
ACCORP Partners template — same structure as other ACCORP clients in this batch
